title: Fix false stale timeout for active procedure runs

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Root cause investigation: procedure d010b142-1b67-48e3-b408-150ab2727e83 did not crash in the direct CLI log; it completed its own stop/finalize path after detecting changed feedback. The task was later marked STALLED and ultimately FAILED with SIGTERM because stale-timeout relies on chat silence and same-host PID checks. Planned fix: add task runtime heartbeat updates from procedure stage/progress events, use heartbeat as a primary activity signal in stale-timeout, and avoid timing out foreign-host runtimes that cannot be verified locally.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementation update: added  at procedure start, refreshes on MCP stage/progress updates, and stale-timeout now prefers the freshest activity signal across chat, heartbeat, and startedAt. Foreign-host runtimes are now skipped instead of being treated as dead. Running focused procedure timeout and transport tests now.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementation update: added runtime heartbeat metadata at procedure start, refreshes on MCP stage and progress updates, and stale-timeout now prefers the freshest activity signal across chat, heartbeat, and startedAt. Foreign-host runtimes are skipped instead of being treated as dead. Focused procedure timeout and transport tests are passing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Procedure-focused validation: replayed the Dosage failure timeline locally against timeout_stale_procedures. Without runtime heartbeat metadata, the same timeline times out as before. With runtime.lastHeartbeatAt injected on the same timeline, the run is skipped as fresh_no_chat_runtime with activity_source runtime_heartbeat. This confirms the fix targets the actual failure mode rather than only satisfying unit tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User requested live procedure validation after the timeout fix. Verifying the direct CLI optimize command shape now so I can launch a fresh Dosage procedure run and observe real procedure/task records.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User requested a fresh live rerun of the Dosage optimizer because several days have passed and the feedback target has changed. Checking for any active Dosage optimizer process first, then launching a new direct CLI run if clear.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User requested immediate stop of Dosage rerun procedure 7b9ac2ae-a9f1-4462-8f27-d82956c3dd4b. Sent SIGINT via direct CLI session, then SIGTERM and finally SIGKILL to wrapper/child processes after they continued running. During shutdown race the optimizer still submitted score versions 69082dbb-4772-4783-9f5e-acb86e768f6c and 29a08d22-c9e7-4a8e-9738-ac566b56507c before process exit. Verifying final task/procedure state now.
